items.title: Target struggles with identity, TJX shines for value and more
items.description: Target (TGT) is getting costs under control, but sales have been flat or falling for over three years; meanwhile, Walmart (WMT) seems to have a clearer identity around low prices. Zacks Investment Management Chief Market Strategist Brian Mulberry joins Market Domination Overtime Host Josh Lipton to explain why Home Depot (HD) has an edge over Lowe's (LOW) and why TJX Companies (TJX) stands out. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.

items.title: Earnings live: Ross Stores stock jumps on upbeat results, CrowdStrike posts slight top- and bottom-line beats
items.description: The S&P 500 was on track for double-digit earnings growth, with more than half of companies having reported Q4 results so far.

items.title: Home Depot Weighs New Stores And AI Tool Against Modest Growth Outlook
items.description: Home Depot (NYSE:HD) plans to open approximately 15 new stores in 2026, expanding its physical footprint. The company is also rolling out an AI-powered project planning tool aimed at professional customers. Both moves focus on strengthening support for pros while adding new brick and mortar capacity. Home Depot, trading at $370.81, remains a key name in big box home improvement, with a 3 year return of 34.2% and a 5 year return of 61.7%. The stock is up 7.2% year to date, even as the 1 year...
